root_cause: check_partial_tp used fill_price + 1.5*risk_distance for both LONG and SHORT. For SHORT trades this level is above fill_price, so current_price <= partial_tp_level fires immediately at entry.
fix_summary: Lines 123-130 of trade_lifecycle.py replaced with direction-aware conditional: LONG uses fill + 1.5*risk_distance (>= check), SHORT uses fill - 1.5*risk_distance (<= check).
edge_cases: SHORT at fill price → False (BVA: at-entry boundary — was True before fix)
SHORT at fill - 1.5*risk_distance → True (exact boundary trigger)
SHORT at fill - 1.5*risk_distance + 0.01 → False (one tick inside boundary)
SHORT at fill - 1.5*risk_distance - 0.01 → True (one tick past boundary)
PARTIALLY_CLOSED state → False (not OPEN, no partial TP check)
LONG unaffected: fill + 1.5*risk_distance still triggers correctly
